framework.name: Dual-actor token (composite identity)
framework.description: Agents need tokens encoding BOTH the user's entitlements AND the agent's service account — effective scope is the intersection. OAuth has no native construct for two distinct actors in one token. This is the 'constrained envelope' problem restated as an identity problem.
framework.raw_quote: its scope is the intersection of the developer's entitlements and the agent's service entitlements. The base OAuth 2.0/2.1 specifications provide no construct for representing two distinct actors within a single token
